column2: Operational Readiness with Copilot and CRE SharePoint
column3: Layering Microsoft 365 Copilot on top of the existing Wilton CRE SharePoint site (The Site Libraries categorize exact building plans, product/asset data, and room information). Leaders and technicians can ask natural‑language questions (e.g., “Show the a table of the rooms that have cleanroom ISO level 5, sort by department and give the total usable square footage") and have Copilot search, reason over, and extract the right drawings, specs, and locations—then produce concise briefings or comparisons in seconds. The result is having the correct information in hand for - accurate incident response, more efficient maintenance, and better decision support for space planning and long‑term studies.
column4:
Our requests would be to use Copilot to reason and find information within a group of libraries in SharePoint. It could be simple Excel analysis and report to finding many types of data for an area of a building where a leak formed. 


Data Reporting 1: Using Excel and SharePoint, instruct Copilot to analyze the dataset and retrieve the matching floor‑plan files from the SharePoint library.

Copilot Prompt: “Create a table that includes room number, building number, floor number, and the floor‑plan link for all conference rooms assigned to the D&E department.”

 

Data Reporting 2: Use Excel to analyze cleanroom allocation by department.

Copilot Prompt: “Analyze the dataset and produce a table showing all departments that occupy cleanroom space. For each department, calculate the total usable square footage by ISO level.”

 

Data Mining 1: Using the SharePoint library, retrieve all files that contain information about the sprinkler system, piping, or fire‑protection components located in Area 6 of Building W77.

Copilot Prompt: “Copilot, search the SharePoint library and list all documents related to the sprinkler system or any fire‑protection components in Building W77, specifically in Area 6.”

 

Data Mining 2: SharePoint library holds the latest Excel file with the occupancy and usage numbers - based on occupancy sensor data for the conference rooms. This scenario we are looking to add rooms to see how much impact each added room would have usage and occupancy.  

Copilot Prompt: “Use the conference‑room data to report seating capacities, average utilization (%), and average people per use. Analyze how adding up to four new 4‑person rooms would affect (a) utilization and (b) average people per use for the current inventory of rooms. Show how each additional room (+1, +2, +3, +4) changes the utilization of the existing rooms compared to baseline.”
column5: ["Increased efficiency","Better decision making","Improved user experience","Improved accuracy"]
column6: We have security in place through group level access to the libraries - we would need to review and verify some libraries stay off limits to Copilot.

column3:
Prompt 1 – Samenvatting overleg + acties

“Vat het overleg van vandaag samen in maximaal 10 bullets. Benoem per actiepunt: actie, eigenaar en deadline (indien bekend).”

Prompt 2 – Inhoud laatste overleg terughalen

“Welke belangrijke beslissingen, open punten en risico’s zijn besproken in het laatste overleg [naam overleg]?”

Prompt 3 – Actiepunten uit meerdere overleggen

“Geef een overzicht van alle openstaande actiepunten uit de laatste 3 overleggen over [projectnaam], gesorteerd op urgentie.”
